title: Artificial Chemist: An Autonomous Quantum Dot Synthesis Bot
first_hand_sources_checked: DOI landing `https://doi.org/10.1002/adma.202001626` -> Wiley; Wiley abstract page `https://advanced.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1002/adma.202001626`; official NC State page `https://news.ncsu.edu/2020/06/artificial-chemist/`; NSF PAR record `https://par.nsf.gov/biblio/10165734`; NSF PAR accepted-manuscript PDF `https://par.nsf.gov/servlets/purl/10165734`
recommended_pdf_url_or_status: Legal full-text available via NSF PAR accepted-manuscript PDF: `https://par.nsf.gov/servlets/purl/10165734`
concrete_scientific_objects: inorganic perovskite quantum dots; target bandgaps; quantum yield; composition polydispersity
concrete_object_evidence: NSF PAR accepted manuscript says the system autonomously synthesizes made-to-measure inorganic perovskite QDs in flow, tunes quantum yield and composition polydispersity at target bandgaps from 1.9 to 2.9 eV, obtains 11 tailored QD compositions within 30 h, and uses closed-loop knowledge transfer to improve target emission accuracy
safety_or_access_note: Ordinary publisher-access friction, not safety-related: Wiley endpoints were Cloudflare-blocked here, but a legal government-hosted accepted-manuscript PDF is openly accessible via NSF PAR
short_reason: Much stronger than the current source-limited packet because a first-hand full manuscript is now accessible; `04` is strongly stabilized and this paper may be eligible for source-limited removal after reviewer adjudication
